content: Usually we are people that contribute regularly to the project one way or another. Sometimes we also have discussions on particular topics with guests.
Am i understand correctly: once someone has been contribute regularly to the project, then can join in the team meeting.
Not necessarily, as I said, sometimes we have particular topics with guests. The difference with the open meeting is that the latter may be less technical and anybody can join anytime.

Right, it's not an exclusive club, so everyone is welcome ;-). But note that the weekly meeting mostly focus on making thing forward-progress: it's organized in a way that active developers can exchange information quickly, and that may not be suitable for everyone.

The open meeting (once every Linux kernel release cycle) on the other hand provides a way to have free-form discussion, technical or not, which hopefully can help everyone.
